title: ARTIS Seafood Consumption Estimates (Midpoint), 1996–2020
abstract: Global, species-disaggregated apparent seafood consumption estimates by consuming country, reconstructed by the ARTIS project from bilateral trade and production data. Tracks the source country, exporter, and consumer for each flow along with habitat, production method, and consumption source (domestic vs. foreign), across five HS classification revisions spanning 1996-2020.

spatial.resolution: national (global consumption estimates; not spatially disaggregated below country level)

resources.name: ARTIS consumption estimates, midpoint (ZIP of 71 CSVs)

variables.name: hs_version
variables.label: HS Classification Version
variables.description: Harmonized System revision used for this record's coding.

variables.name: exporter_iso3c
variables.label: Exporter
variables.description: Exporting country in the trade chain leading to consumption; may be null for direct domestic consumption.

variables.name: consumer_iso3c
variables.label: Consuming Country
variables.description: Country where the product is estimated to be consumed.

variables.name: dom_source
variables.label: Domestic/Foreign Source
variables.description: Whether the consumed product originated domestically or from a foreign source.

variables.name: sciname
variables.label: Scientific Name
variables.description: Taxon resolved for the consumed product.

variables.name: habitat
variables.label: Habitat
variables.description: Marine or inland habitat of the source species.

variables.name: consumption_source
variables.label: Consumption Source
variables.description: Domestic or foreign classification of the consumption pathway.

variables.name: sciname_hs_modified
variables.label: Scientific Name (HS-modified)
variables.description: Scientific name adjusted for HS-code resolution; used because this file omits the HS6 code to reduce file size (per attributes_README).

variables.name: consumption_live_t
variables.label: Consumption (Live Weight)
variables.description: Estimated apparent consumption in live-weight equivalent.

access.how_to_request: ARTIS is a research-consortium data product understood to be openly published; exact access portal/terms were not verified locally. Local ZIP copy available on request from the KAUST Data Inventory Team pending confirmation of redistribution terms.

provenance.originator: ARTIS (Aquatic Resource Trade in Species) research consortium

known_issues: The ZIP contains 71 separate CSVs, one per HS-classification/year combination, mirroring trade.zip's structure; coverage years overlap across HS revisions, so the same de-duplication caution applies before aggregating.
n_rows (~65 million) is an order-of-magnitude estimate derived from total uncompressed size (~6.65 GB) and average observed row length; not computed exactly.

Global dataset, not filtered to Saudi Arabia or the Red Sea; must be filtered on consumer_iso3c / exporter_iso3c / source_country_iso3c = 'SAU' for Kingdom-relevant subsets, and even then cannot distinguish Red Sea vs. Arabian Gulf.
This file omits the HS6 code (present in trade.zip) to reduce file size, substituting 'sciname_hs_modified'; joins to product descriptions in attribute_tables.zip must account for this.
Requires joining with the 'artis-attribute-tables' source for population data (per-capita consumption), taxonomic resolution, and country groupings.
Originator/license/DOI not independently verified — see the same caveat on the 'artis-seafood-trade-matrix' source.
ZIP also contains macOS resource-fork artifacts (__MACOSX/ entries) alongside the 71 real data files.
